parameters.name: TimeRange
parameters.label: Time Range
parameters.description: Time window used by all queries in this workbook.

parameters.name: Model
parameters.label: Model (optional)
parameters.description: Filter panels to a specific model name (e.g. gpt-4o). Use All for no filter.

content.json:
# AI Usage Dashboard

> **Stub status (as of 2026-03-20):** All KQL panels are fully wired to live Application Insights data. Results will be empty in environments where the backend has not emitted the relevant telemetry events yet (e.g., before the first AI-backed narration call). Every panel carries an info banner explaining which event to look for. No portal-only drift risk: the workbook JSON is versioned in `infrastructure/workbooks/ai-usage-dashboard.workbook.json` and deployed via Bicep.

Token consumption, cost tracking, and narration/tool invocation counts sourced entirely from **backend-emitted telemetry** in Application Insights. No MCP-client-side counters are used; the backend policy and validation layers are the authoritative source per the narration-first posture.

## When to open this workbook

- Spend is unexpectedly high or rising and you want to identify the responsible model or operation.
- Token counts are growing faster than narration volume — indicates prompt inflation or cache bypass.
- `AI.Cost.SoftThresholdCrossed` alert fired — use **Budget / Limit Events** to confirm scope.
- Reviewing whether prompt-template versions in production match the hashes in `shared/src/prompts/` — use **Prompt Template Retrievals**.
- Assessing MCP narration throughput after a deployment or traffic change.
- Investigating hero-prose generation failures — use **Hero Prose Event Counts** for quick triage, then switch to the AI Operations Dashboard for per-failure-reason breakdown.

## Telemetry covered

All event names are from the canonical `GAME_EVENT_NAMES` registry in `shared/src/telemetryEvents.ts`:

| Event | Panel | What it tells you |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `AI.Cost.WindowSummary` | Cost Window Summary | Aggregated spend and operation count per window, per model |
| `AI.Cost.Estimated` | Token Usage by Model | Pre-call prompt + completion token estimates |
| `AI.Cost.SoftThresholdCrossed` | Budget / Limit Events | Soft budget warning (does not block the call) |
| `AI.Cost.InputCapped` | Budget / Limit Events | Input truncated to max token limit |
| `AI.Cost.InputAdjusted` | Budget / Limit Events | Input size reduced to fit cost constraints |
| `AI.Cost.OverrideRejected` | Budget / Limit Events | Cost override attempt rejected by policy |
| `Description.Hero.*` | Hero Prose Event Counts | Cache hit/miss/generate/failure outcomes |
| `Description.Hero.GenerateSuccess` | Hero Prose Token Usage | Actual token counts from Azure AI Foundry API response |
| `MCP.Tool.Invoked` | MCP Tool Invocations | Narration/tool call counts and latency by tool name |
| `PromptTemplate.Get` | Prompt Template Retrievals | Template id, version, hash — detect drift from `shared/src/prompts/` |

## What to expect per panel

**Cost Window Summary** — One row per model. In steady state expect one active model (`hero-prose`). Multiple models indicate parallel experimentation; validate intentional.

**Token Usage by Model** — `TotalPromptTokens` grows with narration volume; `TotalCompletionTokens` stays proportionally smaller. If completion tokens are ≥ prompt tokens investigate prompt truncation or generation-loop issues.

**Budget / Limit Events** — In healthy operation this panel should show zero rows. Any `AI.Cost.SoftThresholdCrossed` rows are advisory. `AI.Cost.InputCapped` or `AI.Cost.OverrideRejected` rows indicate active throttling.

**Hero Prose Event Counts** — Healthy: `CacheHit` count >> `GenerateSuccess` count. High `CacheMiss` + low `GenerateSuccess` may indicate cache invalidation or generation failures; cross-check with `GenerateFailure` count and switch to the AI Operations Dashboard for root-cause.

**Hero Prose Token Usage** — More accurate than pre-call estimates (uses actual API response). Use `AvgTokens` as the baseline; a sustained increase usually means longer location descriptions.

**MCP Tool Invocations** — p95 latency under 2 000 ms is healthy. Spikes above 5 000 ms warrant investigation in the AI Operations Dashboard (MCP Throttles section).

**Prompt Template Retrievals** — In steady state a single `Version` and `Hash` per `TemplateId`. Multiple hashes for the same template id indicate version drift; verify the canonical hash in `shared/src/prompts/templates/`.

## Non-goals

- World simulation metrics (NPC counts, realm area counts) → use the Agent Sandbox Dashboard.
- Hero prose failure root-cause (timeout / config-missing / invalid-response breakdown) → use the AI Operations Dashboard.
- Agent step latency and proposal outcomes → use the Agent Sandbox Dashboard.

## Related dashboards

| Dashboard | What it adds |
| --- | --- |
| AI Operations Dashboard | Hero prose failure reasons, MCP auth decisions, throttle/failure rates |
| Agent Sandbox Dashboard | Agent step lifecycle, proposal validation outcomes, DLQ volume |

**References:** [shared/src/telemetryEvents.ts](https://github.com/piquet-h/the-shifting-atlas/blob/main/shared/src/telemetryEvents.ts) · [docs/observability.md](https://github.com/piquet-h/the-shifting-atlas/blob/main/docs/observability.md) · [infrastructure/README.md](https://github.com/piquet-h/the-shifting-atlas/blob/main/infrastructure/README.md)

parameters.name: TimeRange
parameters.label: Time Range
parameters.description: Time window used by all queries in this workbook.

parameters.name: Model
parameters.label: Model (optional)
parameters.description: Filter panels to a specific model. Use All for no filter.
parameters.query:
customEvents
| where timestamp > ago(7d)
| where name in ('AI.Cost.Estimated', 'Description.Hero.GenerateSuccess')
| extend model = tostring(customDimensions['model'])
| where isnotempty(model)
| distinct model
| order by model asc

content.json:
---

## AI Cost Window Summaries

Aggregated cost summaries emitted by the backend cost-tracking layer (`AI.Cost.WindowSummary`).
Each event captures total estimated spend and operation count for the window. The `AI.Cost.Estimated`
event is emitted per call with `promptTokens`, `completionTokens`, and `estimatedUsd`.

> **Note:** Estimates are computed client-side before the call; actual billed tokens may differ. Use windowed summaries for trend analysis rather than exact billing reconciliation.

content.query:
let startTime = {TimeRange:start};
let modelRaw = '{Model:escape}';
let modelFilter = iff(modelRaw == '' or modelRaw == '{Model:escape}' or modelRaw == 'All', '', modelRaw);
customEvents
| where timestamp > startTime
| where name == 'AI.Cost.WindowSummary'
| extend ic = tolong(coalesce(itemCount, 1))
| extend
    model = tostring(customDimensions['model']),
    operationCount = tolong(customDimensions['operationCount']),
    totalUsd = todouble(customDimensions['totalEstimatedUsd'])
| where modelFilter == '' or model == modelFilter
| summarize
    Windows = sum(ic),
    TotalOperations = sum(operationCount),
    TotalUsd = round(sum(totalUsd), 4)
  by Model = model
| order by TotalUsd desc
content.title: Cost Window Summary by Model
content.noDataMessage: No AI.Cost.WindowSummary events in the selected time range. Cost window summaries are emitted by the backend AI cost-tracking layer; verify that backend telemetry is flowing to this Application Insights resource.

content.query:
let startTime = {TimeRange:start};
let modelRaw = '{Model:escape}';
let modelFilter = iff(modelRaw == '' or modelRaw == '{Model:escape}' or modelRaw == 'All', '', modelRaw);
customEvents
| where timestamp > startTime
| where name == 'AI.Cost.Estimated'
| extend ic = tolong(coalesce(itemCount, 1))
| extend
    model = tostring(customDimensions['model']),
    promptTokens = tolong(customDimensions['promptTokens']),
    completionTokens = tolong(customDimensions['completionTokens']),
    estimatedUsd = todouble(customDimensions['estimatedUsd'])
| where modelFilter == '' or model == modelFilter
| summarize
    Calls = sum(ic),
    TotalPromptTokens = sum(promptTokens),
    TotalCompletionTokens = sum(completionTokens),
    TotalUsd = round(sum(estimatedUsd), 4)
  by Model = model
| order by TotalUsd desc
content.title: Token Usage by Model (per-call estimates)
content.noDataMessage: No AI.Cost.Estimated events in the selected time range.

content.query:
let startTime = {TimeRange:start};
let modelRaw = '{Model:escape}';
let modelFilter = iff(modelRaw == '' or modelRaw == '{Model:escape}' or modelRaw == 'All', '', modelRaw);
customEvents
| where timestamp > startTime
| where name in ('AI.Cost.SoftThresholdCrossed', 'AI.Cost.InputCapped', 'AI.Cost.InputAdjusted', 'AI.Cost.OverrideRejected')
| extend ic = tolong(coalesce(itemCount, 1))
| extend
    model = tostring(customDimensions['model'])
| where modelFilter == '' or model == modelFilter
| summarize Count = sum(ic) by EventName = name, Model = model
| order by Count desc
content.title: Budget / Limit Events
content.noDataMessage: No budget or limit events (AI.Cost.SoftThresholdCrossed, AI.Cost.InputCapped, AI.Cost.InputAdjusted, AI.Cost.OverrideRejected) in the selected time range. This is the expected state during normal operation.

content.json:
---

## Narration Token Usage (Hero Prose)

Token consumption reported by `Description.Hero.GenerateSuccess` events. Each event carries
`tokenUsage` from the actual Azure AI Foundry / OpenAI API response, making this panel more
accurate than the pre-call `AI.Cost.Estimated` estimates above.

Cache hits (`Description.Hero.CacheHit`) produce no tokens; the **Cache Hit Rate** panel shows
how effectively the cache is reducing model load.

content.query:
let startTime = {TimeRange:start};
let modelRaw = '{Model:escape}';
let modelFilter = iff(modelRaw == '' or modelRaw == '{Model:escape}' or modelRaw == 'All', '', modelRaw);
customEvents
| where timestamp > startTime
| where name in ('Description.Hero.GenerateSuccess', 'Description.Hero.CacheHit', 'Description.Hero.CacheMiss', 'Description.Hero.GenerateFailure')
| extend ic = tolong(coalesce(itemCount, 1))
| extend
    model = tostring(customDimensions['model']),
    event = name
| where modelFilter == '' or model == modelFilter
| summarize Count = sum(ic) by EventName = event
| order by Count desc
content.title: Hero Prose Event Counts
content.noDataMessage: No Description.Hero.* events in the selected time range.

content.query:
let startTime = {TimeRange:start};
let modelRaw = '{Model:escape}';
let modelFilter = iff(modelRaw == '' or modelRaw == '{Model:escape}' or modelRaw == 'All', '', modelRaw);
customEvents
| where timestamp > startTime
| where name == 'Description.Hero.GenerateSuccess'
| extend ic = tolong(coalesce(itemCount, 1))
| extend
    model = tostring(customDimensions['model']),
    tokenUsage = tolong(customDimensions['tokenUsage'])
| where modelFilter == '' or model == modelFilter
| where isnotnull(tokenUsage) and tokenUsage >= 0
| summarize
    Calls = sum(ic),
    TotalTokens = sum(tokenUsage),
    AvgTokens = round(avg(tokenUsage), 0),
    MaxTokens = max(tokenUsage)
  by Model = model
| order by TotalTokens desc
content.title: Hero Prose Token Usage by Model
content.noDataMessage: No Description.Hero.GenerateSuccess events with tokenUsage in the selected time range.

content.json:
---

## MCP Tool Invocations

Narration and tool-use counts surfaced via `MCP.Tool.Invoked` events emitted by the backend
MCP layer. Auth decisions (`MCP.Auth.Allowed` / `MCP.Auth.Denied`) and throttle/failure events
are covered in the AI Operations Dashboard; this panel focuses on invocation volume and latency
to track narration throughput.

> **Authority note:** Only backend-emitted `MCP.*` events are used here. Counters from external
> MCP client runtimes are not considered authoritative.

content.query:
let startTime = {TimeRange:start};
customEvents
| where timestamp > startTime
| where name == 'MCP.Tool.Invoked'
| extend ic = tolong(coalesce(itemCount, 1))
| extend
    toolName = tostring(customDimensions['toolName']),
    latencyMs = tolong(customDimensions['latencyMs'])
| summarize
    Invocations = sum(ic),
    P50Ms = round(percentile(latencyMs, 50), 0),
    P95Ms = round(percentile(latencyMs, 95), 0),
    AvgMs = round(avg(latencyMs), 0)
  by Tool = toolName
| order by Invocations desc
content.title: MCP Tool Invocations by Tool
content.noDataMessage: No MCP.Tool.Invoked events in the selected time range. MCP tool telemetry is emitted by the backend MCP handler layer; verify that the MCP server Function App is connected to this Application Insights resource.

content.json:
---

## Prompt Template Registry

Each call to `PromptTemplate.Get` emits `templateId`, `version`, `hash`, and `cached` dimensions.
Use this panel to detect version drift or unexpected template IDs in production. The canonical
hashes are maintained in `shared/src/prompts/` and verified at build time.

> **Stub note:** Query results depend on `PromptTemplate.Get` being emitted in production.
> No data is expected in environments where template retrieval has not been exercised.

content.query:
let startTime = {TimeRange:start};
customEvents
| where timestamp > startTime
| where name == 'PromptTemplate.Get'
| extend ic = tolong(coalesce(itemCount, 1))
| extend
    templateId = tostring(customDimensions['templateId']),
    version = tostring(customDimensions['version']),
    hash = tostring(customDimensions['hash']),
    cached = tobool(customDimensions['cached'])
| summarize
    Calls = sum(ic),
    CachedHits = sumif(ic, cached == true)
  by TemplateId = templateId, Version = version, Hash = hash
| order by Calls desc
content.title: Prompt Template Retrievals
content.noDataMessage: No PromptTemplate.Get events in the selected time range. These events are emitted when the backend retrieves a prompt template from the registry in shared/src/prompts/.
